title: Moraea radians
intro: Moraea radians, also known as Cape tulip or wild tulip, is a flowering plant species within the family Iridaceae, native to the southern regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Moraea radians include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Moraea radians are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
